name: ConvertString
title: ConvertString
syntax: long ConvertString(byte output[], long& encodedSize, long maxOutputSize, dword outputCodepage, byte input[], long inputSize, dword inputCodepage);
description: Converts a string from one encoding to another encoding. The length of the converted string depends on the requested encoding. The number of bytes used in the output byte array is written to encodedSize. If the size of the output array maxOutputSize is not sufficient to hold the converted string and a terminating “\0”, the function returns an error and the content of output is undefined. Characters that cannot be represented in the requested encoding, are replaced with the best matching character, selected by the Windows function WideCharToMultiByte.

parameters.name: encodedSize
parameters.description: ConvertString writes the number of bytes used in output (including the terminating “\0”) to this parameter.

parameters.name: outputCodepage
parameters.description: Windows code page number for output.The include file Encoding.cin defines the following code pages: CP_UTF8 CP_UTF16 CP_LATIN1 CP_SHIFT_JIS

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The input string in encoding codepage, without BOM.

parameters.name: inputSize
parameters.description: Length of the input string in bytes.

parameters.name: inputCodepage
parameters.description: Windows code page number for input.The include file Encoding.cin defines the following code pages: CP_UTF8 CP_UTF16 CP_LATIN1 CP_SHIFT_JIS

return: ConvertString writes the number of bytes used in
availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
